behaviors.description: The review input is scoped to the lines that changed, not the full content of each modified file.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is module handoff: diff extraction/scope logic must pass only changed hunks into review-input construction. A unit test of a single helper can miss wiring errors between diff parser and payload builder. Rejected System because behavior text does not require real network/OS/subprocess; fixture diff payloads are sufficient. Rejected Agentic/Workflow because correctness is about deterministic scoping, not model judgment.
behaviors.test_description: Run the PR-diff ingestion module and review-input builder together with synthetic multi-file diffs (changed + unchanged regions) and assert the produced review input contains only changed lines/hunks.

behaviors.description: Inline comments are attached to the correct positions in the diff view on the pull request.
behaviors.justification: Real failure boundary is external GitHub diff-position semantics over real HTTP. In-process mocks often accept invalid line/side/commit mappings that fail or mis-anchor on a real PR. Rejected Integration because behavior text requires correctness in the PR diff view, which depends on platform behavior.
behaviors.test_description: Against a real test PR, post inline comments using computed positions and verify via API/UI fetch that each comment is anchored to the intended changed line in the diff.

behaviors.description: The agent identifies at least one real defect in a pull request that contains a known introduced bug.
behaviors.justification: Correctness depends on real model output quality (defect detection). Deterministic stubs hide the exact failure this behavior targets. Rejected Workflow because behavior only requires at least one correct model judgment, not validating multiple agentic decisions in sequence.
behaviors.test_description: Use a real PR fixture with a seeded known bug, run live analysis with the production model, and assert at least one finding correctly describes that bug.

behaviors.description: Every finding listed in the top-level summary corresponds to a posted inline comment ID from the same run.
behaviors.justification: Failure is cross-module state linkage (inline-post results -> summary builder). This is not a single-function invariant, and mocks can still catch mismatched ID propagation if modules are wired together. Rejected System: real network is not strictly required to catch this mapping bug; deterministic fake post responses with run-scoped IDs are enough. Rejected Agentic/Workflow: no model-output-quality dependence.
behaviors.test_description: Execute posting-orchestration modules with a fake comment-post adapter returning unique IDs, then assert each summary item references an ID emitted in that same run and none from prior runs.

behaviors.description: No inline finding is dropped from the final summary payload before posting.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is handoff/aggregation across modules (finding collection -> summary payload). A pure unit test of one formatter can miss losses introduced in orchestration. Rejected System because dropping can be detected without real HTTP/OS by wiring modules in-process. Rejected Agentic/Workflow because this checks completeness propagation, not model judgment.
behaviors.test_description: Run the in-process pipeline from inline-finding list through summary payload generation with varied finding counts and edge metadata; assert one-to-one preservation before post call.

behaviors.description: The automation sequence (read diff, post inline comments, post summary) succeeds for a real pull request when provided a fixed precomputed finding set.
behaviors.justification: Behavior explicitly requires a real pull request and end-to-end API sequence, so real HTTP/platform behavior is part of the failure boundary. Rejected Integration due explicit real-PR requirement. Rejected Workflow because findings are precomputed (no required agentic decision chain).
behaviors.test_description: Run the automation against a real repository PR with fixed findings injected, verify diff read, inline post calls, and final summary post all succeed and are visible on the PR.

behaviors.description: The full live run with model-generated findings (read diff, analyze, post inline comments, post summary) completes correctly for a real pull request without manual intervention.
behaviors.justification: This is a full agent pipeline combining live model-generated analysis and downstream actions; correctness depends on sequenced steps with at least one real model decision plus subsequent handoffs/posting. Rejected Agentic because behavior text covers complete multi-stage run, not just isolated model output quality.
behaviors.test_description: Execute the production pipeline on a real PR with live model analysis enabled; assert autonomous completion, valid inline comments, and coherent summary publication in one uninterrupted run.
